stories.title: Tennessee Sweeps Georgia 2-1 — Lady Vols Are WCWS-Bound (Tier 1)

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Tennessee is going to the WCWS.

Sage Mardjetko. Complete game. 2-1 in Game 2.

The Lady Vols swept Georgia in Knoxville and are heading to Devon Park for the 2nd straight year. 🥎 #LadyVols

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
10th WCWS in program history.

2nd straight year.

47-10 season record.

Tennessee just swept their Super Regional and they are one of the best teams in this tournament. Devon Park better be ready. 🥎 #LadyVols

facebook.long_form:
🥎 TENNESSEE IS GOING TO THE WCWS 🥎

They did it again.

Sage Mardjetko walked out to the circle Friday night in Knoxville, held Georgia to four hits and one run, and Tennessee finished off the Super Regional sweep with a 2-1 win in Game 2.

One night after Karlyn Pickens struck out 10 in a complete-game Game 1 victory, Mardjetko closed it out in Game 2. Back-to-back complete games from two different pitchers. A 2-0 sweep in their own building. The Lady Vols didn't let Georgia breathe in this series.

Tennessee is heading to the Women's College World Series for the 10th time in program history and the second straight year. They finish the regular season and tournament run at 47-10.

Georgia fought. Their 41-20 season says they had every right to be in Knoxville. But Tennessee was simply the better team across two games — and Mardjetko was exactly what the Lady Vols needed when Pickens stepped off the mound.

Devon Park opens May 28. Tennessee will be ready.

🧡 WCWS | Devon Park OKC | May 28 | Tennessee is IN 🥎

[Lady Vols fans — how far does this team go in OKC? Tell us below!] 👇

---
facebook.image_caption: Tennessee sweeps Georgia in the Super Regional — 3-1 Game 1, 2-1 Game 2. The Lady Vols are WCWS-bound for the 10th time and the second straight year. Devon Park, here they come. 🥎 #LadyVols

stories.title: Mississippi State Stuns No. 1 Oklahoma 11-9 — Wells Hits HRs 38 and 39 (Tier 1)

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Kendall Wells hit 2 home runs and drove in 6 runs.

Oklahoma still lost.

Mississippi State scored 5 in the 7th and stunned the No. 1 seed 11-9. 🤯

Game 2 is TODAY at 1 PM ET in Norman. #Sooners #HailState

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Wells: 4-for-5. HR No. 38. HR No. 39. 6 RBIs.

And she's on the losing side.

The greatest individual performance to come up short in Super Regional history? Miss State leads 1-0. Game 2 now. 🥎 #Sooners

facebook.long_form:
🤯 ONE OF THE GREATEST UPSETS IN SUPER REGIONAL HISTORY 🤯

Let's talk about what happened Friday in Norman.

No. 1 Oklahoma. Home field. Love's Field packed. Kendall Wells in the lineup. Every reason to think the Sooners would handle this.

And then Kendall Wells went 4-for-5 with two home runs and six RBIs. Oklahoma held leads of four runs — twice. And somehow, Mississippi State scored five runs in the top of the seventh inning to win 11-9.

This was not a fluke. This was a college softball team executing under the highest possible pressure in the hostile environment of Norman, Oklahoma, and refusing to go away.

Wells hit her 38th home run in the third inning. She hit her 39th in the sixth. By any measure, she had one of the most dominant individual games in Super Regional history. And Oklahoma still lost.

Mississippi State leads the series 1-0. Game 2 is TODAY at 1 PM ET in Norman.

The No. 1 seed has never looked more vulnerable. And one of the most dramatic stories of this tournament has a Game 2 to finish.

🐶 Game 2 | Saturday, May 23 | 1 PM ET | Norman, OK

[Who wins Game 2 today — Oklahoma bounces back or Miss State finishes the upset?] 👇

---
facebook.image_caption: Mississippi State stuns No. 1 Oklahoma 11-9 in Super Regional Game 1. Kendall Wells hit HRs 38 and 39 — and it still wasn't enough. Five-run 7th inning. Miss State leads 1-0. Game 2 today at 1 PM ET. 🤯🥎 #HailState #Sooners

stories.title: Wells (39) vs. Grant (40) — The All-Time HR Race Is One Swing Away (Tier 2)

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Megan Grant: 40 HRs (all-time NCAA record)
Kendall Wells: 39 HRs (2nd all-time)

One swing apart.

Both play today. This is the best individual race in college softball history. 🏆🥎 #Sooners #GoBruins

facebook.long_form:
���� THE GREATEST HOME RUN RACE IN COLLEGE SOFTBALL HISTORY — AND IT'S HAPPENING RIGHT NOW 🏆

College softball has never seen this before.

Megan Grant (UCLA) entered this Super Regional with 40 home runs — the most in NCAA softball history. She broke the old mark of 37 in spectacular fashion, turning it into a record she now fully owns.

And then Friday happened.

Kendall Wells hit her 38th and 39th home runs of the season in Oklahoma's Super Regional Game 1. Just like that, Wells is at 39 — second all-time in NCAA softball history. One home run behind Grant.

Both players are still competing this weekend. Wells plays in Game 2 today against Mississippi State at 1 PM ET. Grant and UCLA face UCF in Los Angeles in Game 2 today.

One swing separates them.

If Wells hits one today, she ties the all-time record. If Grant extends her lead, she builds more cushion. Both players are making history in real time, and the tournament is the backdrop.

College softball does not get more dramatic than this.

🥎 Wells: 39 HRs | Grant: 40 HRs | One HR apart | Both play today

[Who do you think hits the big one today — Wells or Grant?] 👇

---
facebook.image_caption: Megan Grant: 40 HRs (all-time NCAA record). Kendall Wells: 39 HRs (2nd all-time). One swing apart. Both play Game 2 today. This is the greatest individual duel in college softball history. 🏆🥎 #Sooners #GoBruins

stories.title: Arizona State Upsets Defending Champion Texas 4-1 in Super Regional Game 1 (Tier 2)

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
No. 19 Arizona State just beat defending national champion Texas 4-1 in Super Regional Game 1.

Texas has to win tonight at 7 PM ET on ESPN or their title defense is over.

The defending champs are on the ropes at home. 🥎 #HookEm #SunDevils

facebook.long_form:
😤 THE DEFENDING CHAMPIONS ARE IN TROUBLE 😤

Texas won the national title last year in Oklahoma City. They came back this year with the same talent, the same coaching staff, and the same home field in Austin. They were the No. 3 seed — expected to cruise through McCombs Field.

On Friday night, No. 19 Arizona State took Game 1, 4-1.

Texas has never been down 0-1 in a Super Regional with more to lose. The defending champion now faces a must-win situation at home tonight.

Arizona State is not here by accident. They earned this Super Regional berth and they played like it on Friday night. The Sun Devils went into Austin and outpitched, outplayed, and out-competed the team with the banner.

Game 2 is tonight at 7 PM ET on ESPN at McCombs Field. Texas wins to force Game 3. Arizona State wins to head to their first WCWS.

The defending champion's season is on the line. Tonight matters.

🤘 Game 2 | Saturday, May 23 | 7 PM ET | ESPN | Austin, TX

[Can Texas rally tonight, or is Arizona State completing the upset?] 👇

---
facebook.image_caption: No. 19 Arizona State beat No. 3 Texas 4-1 in Super Regional Game 1. The defending national champions trail at home. Game 2 tonight at 7 PM ET on ESPN. Everything is on the line. 😤🥎 #HookEm #SunDevils

stories.title: Saturday's Super Regional Game 2 Slate — Seven Series Decide Who Goes to WCWS (Tier 2)

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Seven Game 2s today. Seven WCWS spots decided.

📺 12:30 PM — Tech vs FL (ABC)
📺 1:00 PM — MSU vs OU
📺 3:00 PM — LSU vs ALA (ESPN)
📺 4:00 PM — Duke vs ARK (ESPN2)
📺 5:00 PM — OkSt vs NEB (ESPN2)
📺 7:00 PM — ASU vs TEX (ESPN)

Set your alarms. 🥎

facebook.long_form:
📅 SATURDAY IS THE BIGGEST DAY IN COLLEGE SOFTBALL — HERE'S YOUR FULL GUIDE 📅

Seven Super Regionals. Seven chances to punch a WCWS ticket. All happening today.

Here's the complete Game 2 schedule:

🕐 12:30 PM ET — Texas Tech at Florida (ABC, Gainesville)
Texas Tech leads 1-0 after winning 10-8 in Game 1. Florida must win to survive.

🕐 1:00 PM ET — Mississippi State at Oklahoma (Norman)
Miss State's stunning 11-9 upset has OU on the ropes. Game 2 in Norman.

🕑 3:00 PM ET — LSU at Alabama (ESPN, Tuscaloosa)
Alabama blanked LSU 7-0 in Game 1. Can Alabama close it out today?

🕑 4:00 PM ET — Duke at Arkansas (ESPN2, Fayetteville)
Arkansas won Game 1 in run-rule fashion (14-5). Deifel's team can reach the WCWS today.

🕔 5:00 PM ET — Oklahoma State at Nebraska (ESPN2, Lincoln)
Nebraska won 8-1 in Game 1 after the weather delay. One win from WCWS.

📺 Evening — UCF at UCLA (Los Angeles)
UCLA won 9-1 in Game 1. UCF needs a win to stay alive.

📺 7:00 PM ET — Arizona State at Texas (ESPN, Austin)
ASU upset Texas 4-1 in Game 1. The defending champs must win tonight.

Seven series. Seven WCWS spots. Everything gets decided today.

[Which game are you most excited to watch?] 👇

---
facebook.image_caption: Seven Super Regional Game 2s today. Seven WCWS spots on the line. Nebraska, Alabama, and Arkansas can clinch. Florida, Oklahoma, Texas, and UCLA must win to survive. It all starts at 12:30 PM ET. 🥎

stories.title: Texas Tech Shocks Florida 10-8 — Gators' Home Mystique Broken (Tier 3)

x_posts.label: Post 6
x_posts.text:
Florida was 36-1 at home in the NCAA Tournament since 2013.

Texas Tech walked into Gainesville and won 10-8.

Mia Williams hit the go-ahead 2-run HR in the 7th.

Game 2 today at 12:30 PM ET, ABC. 🥎 #Sooners #WreckEm

facebook.long_form:
📊 FLORIDA WAS 36-1 IN HOME NCAA TOURNAMENT GAMES SINCE 2013. 📊

Friday, that became 36-2.

Texas Tech went to Gainesville — one of the most imposing home environments in college softball — and beat Florida 10-8 in Super Regional Game 1. The Red Raiders trailed in the seventh inning and found a way to win on the road.

Mia Williams was the hero. Her go-ahead two-run home run in the top of the seventh gave Texas Tech the lead they wouldn't give back.

Florida's 36-1 home tournament record wasn't just a number. It was a psychological edge — every team that walked into Katie Seashole Pressly Stadium knew that number, and it weighted every at-bat, every decision, every moment.

Texas Tech apparently doesn't read the stat sheet.

Game 2 is today at 12:30 PM ET on ABC. Florida needs to win to survive. Texas Tech needs to win to complete one of the tournament's great upsets.

The mystique is gone. What replaces it today?

🔴 Game 2 | Saturday, May 23 | 12:30 PM ET | ABC | Gainesville, FL

[Florida fans — is the home magic gone or can the Gators respond today?] 👇

---
facebook.image_caption: Florida was 36-1 at home in the NCAA Tournament since 2013. Texas Tech won 10-8 in Game 1. Mia Williams hit the go-ahead 2-run HR in the 7th. The mystique is broken. Game 2 today at 12:30 PM ET on ABC. 🥎 #WreckEm #Gators
